title: Behavioral: Success & Failure (STAR Method)
body:
🏆 Success Story:

Situation: The business problem or context.

Task: Your specific role and objective.

Action: What YOU did (focus on "I" instead of "We", how you aligned the team, how you utilized data).

Result: Numbers. (Revenue generated, time saved, specific metric improvement).


📉 Failure / Conflict Story:

Situation/Task: A feature launch that failed or a conflict with R&D.

Action: How you identified the failure (Analytics!) and how you handled it with management and the team.

Result: The consequences and impact.


Learning: The most crucial part - what you learned and how you have adjusted your working process since then.

title: Product Sense:
body:
1. Clarify & Context: Ask questions! What is the goal? (Revenue, Engagement, Retention?). Who is the main competitor? Are there technical constraints?


2. Target Audience: Who are the users? (e.g., Solo travelers, families, budget backpackers). Select ONE primary persona.


3. User Pain Points/Needs: List 2-3 specific problems this persona faces. Prioritize the biggest problem.


4. Brainstorm Solutions: Propose 3 solutions (1 safe, 1 innovative, 1 "moonshot"). Choose the best one based on Impact vs. Effort.


5. Metrics & Trade-offs: How will we measure success? (Primary metric, Secondary metric, Counter-metric). What are the risks?

title: Product Analytics:
body:
1. Clarify the Metric: Is it a sudden drop or gradual? Web or App (versions)? Specific region (e.g., only in Japan) or global? iOS or Android? 


2. Internal Factors:


  • Data tracking broken? (Check analytics pipeline).
  • Recent deployments/releases? (Did a new feature break the flow?).
  • Cannibalization? (Did a new promotion steal traffic from this funnel?).


3. External Factors:


  • Technical issues outside our control? (AWS outage, payment gateway down).
  • Seasonality or Holidays? (Compare to other periods)
  • Competitors? (Did Booking.com or Expedia launch a massive campaign?).
  • Macro events? (Weather, political events impacting travel).


4. Action Plan: How to verify the hypothesis and steps to fix it.
